title: Fix Google access denied page — replace Google with DuckDuckGo search backend

requirements.text: Ensure the backend HTTP outcall targets the DuckDuckGo API endpoint and does not make any requests to Google or any URL that would trigger a Google OAuth/access page. Verify the constructed URL in the Motoko actor points to DuckDuckGo (e.g., api.duckduckgo.com) and remove any Google-related URLs.

quotes: It has a google page that tells me i dont have access to the page

acceptanceCriteria: The Motoko actor constructs HTTP outcall URLs pointing only to the DuckDuckGo API.
No Google domains appear in any backend HTTP request URL.
Searching returns DuckDuckGo results without redirecting to a Google access-denied page.

requirements.text: In the InAppBrowser component, when an iframe fails to load or is blocked (e.g., X-Frame-Options denial), display a user-friendly message with a button to open the URL in a new tab instead of showing a broken or empty iframe. Detect iframe load errors and show a fallback UI immediately.

quotes: It has a google page that tells me i dont have access to the page

acceptanceCriteria: If an iframe returns an access-denied or blocked response, the user sees a clear error message inside the InAppBrowser modal.
A visible 'Open in new tab' button is shown as a fallback whenever iframe content cannot be displayed.
The fallback is triggered automatically on iframe load error without requiring user action beyond clicking the button.

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
nonGoals: Adding Google Search or any Google API integration.
Implementing OAuth or any third-party authentication flow.
Changing the overall app design or color theme.
